cards.back: A distributed digital ledger technology using cryptography to record transactions and manage data across a network of computers.
A theorem in distributed systems that states it is impossible for a distributed system to simultaneously guarantee all three of consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.
A concept in distributed systems that describes the challenge of achieving agreement among nodes in a network when some nodes may be malicious or Byzantine.
A mechanism by which nodes in a distributed system agree on the state of the system, often achieved through voting or other forms of negotiation.

cards.front: What is the purpose of Chapter 1 in Blockchain Technology?
cards.back: To introduce the history of blockchain, explaining its origins and evolution as a technology.
